title: Rectal bleeding surgical review

red_flags.text: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.
red_flags.rationale: Supports bleeding, bowel habit, prolapse, thrombosis, prior intervention and focused examination context for rectal bleeding surgical review; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids

red_flags.text: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.
red_flags.rationale: Supports recommendation-qualified rectal bleeding, bowel change, pain, weight loss, anaemia and rectal-mass context for rectal bleeding surgical review; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Bleeding amount and pattern, haemodynamic status, medicine context, full anorectal and abdominal examination, laboratory, endoscopy, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, referral and urgency remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for rectal bleeding surgical review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
